title: so it goes
summary: Rúben plans their Valentine’s date, but then all of his cleverly thought out plans fail, and it looks like this is going to go down in history as the worst Valentine’s Day ever. John “punishes” him in his own way.
body:
The roses start dying at midday. Well – they’re not actually dying, but that’s what it looks like to Rúben when he walks back into the kitchen and finds his perfect, deep-red bouquet listing in the vase. One of the stem has slipped, the head drooping over the rim, petals pressed against the glass.

“Great,” he mutters. “Of course today.”

He’d trimmed them at an angle, used the little packet of flower food like the woman in the shop had told him. He rearranged them twice until they looked perfect. And now they’re collapsing. 

The timer on the oven beeps then. He goes over and pulls the door open. When he does, the lasagna is… not golden. In fact, it’s black.

“Fuck off,” he says, staring at it. “No. No, no, no.”

He doesn’t understand how this could’ve happened. He followed the recipe exactly to the doot, measured everything. He even checked halfway through. And now there’s only this sad, burnt rectangle that smells like the inside of a chimney.

The plan, in his head, is simple. It has been simple for weeks.

Breakfast in bed, with flowers. Then the museum John’s been on about but never had time for. Then an early dinner at the little place on the corner that agreed to close just for them. Walk by the river after, fairy lights and all, then home for dessert and yeah. Perfect. Thoughtful. Romantic, without being sickly.

But here he is, with dead roses and lasagna burnt to shit.

His phone buzzes on the counter. He wipes his hands on a towel and picks it up.

J 🩵: Leaving now!! Be home in 15. Love you rubes x

He stares at the little blue heart until he breathes a bit. Okay. It’s one burnt meal, everything else can still go right. 

He shoves the lasagna tin onto the stovetop, opens the fridge and eyes the emergency option he’d hidden at the back. A high-end ready meal. He’d wanted to do it all himself this time, homemade from scratch, but life has other plans it seems. He rips the packaging open, chucks it in the oven, sets a new timer and then goes back to the flowers, turning them so the best sides face out. Just in time, too, because he hears the front door open a moment later.

“Love?” John calls.

“In here,” he answers, trying not to sound like a man who has just murdered lasagna.

John appears in the doorway, grocery bag in one hand. He grins when he sees the roses, eyes going wide. 

“Woah,” he lights up, stepping closer. “Look at you. House smells nice.”

It smells like burnt cheese, but sure.

Rúben leans in to kiss him. “Happy Valentine’s.”

“You too,” John noses at his cheek for a second, then pulls back to inspect the bouquet, grazing a velvety petal. “These are gorgeous. You didn’t have to do all this, y’know.”

“I wanted to,” he says. “This is just the beginning.”

John quirks a brow, smirking. “Should I be worried?”

“Only a little,” he taps John’s arse. “Go shower, kitten. We’re on a tight schedule.”

“Oh my God, there’s an actual schedule?” John laughs, all bright. “You’re ridiculous. I love you.”

And just like that, despite the charred disaster in the bin and the droopy flowers, his day feels salvageable.

For about thirteen minutes.

He’s plating up the ready meal upgrade. Trying to make it look homemade, if not for his own pride then for John, when the lights flicker. Then flicker again. Then, dramatically, the whole flat goes dark with a heavy click somewhere in the walls. 

There’s stunned silence, and then from down the hall, John’s voice going “Hello?”

“Don’t move!” Rúben yells back, already moving. His shin slams into a chair. “Fuck! Fuck–”

He manages to find the counter with his hand, then the drawer and the little torch they keep for exactly this kind of thing. He flicks it on, a weak yellow circle cutting through the dark.

John appears out of the bathroom doorway, a towel wrapped around his waist. “Bit early for mood lighting though, innit? Thank God I haven’t stepped in the shower.”

“Power’s gone,” Rúben says flatly. “And the food is in the oven.”

“Oh. That’s not ideal.”

Rúben goes to check anyway, because maybe the universe is feeling kind today. Well it isn’t. The oven’s stopped mid-cook, the food visibly half-done. If he takes it out now, it’ll be cold slop. If he waits… well, he can’t, because the oven is dead.

He stares at it, torch beam shaking just a tiny bit in his hand. He wants to cry.

“Okay,” John says from behind him, “deep breaths.”

“I planned this,” he says, a bit blank. “I planned the timings. We had to eat now so we could get to the museum and then the restaurant and then–”

“Hey,” John steps up, sliding his hand between Rúben’s shoulders. “Hey. It’s not your fault someone knocked the power out.”

“I should’ve checked,” he says, irrational but fixed on the idea nonetheless. “I should’ve thought of this, should’ve had something cold ready or, or–”

“Right, and you didn’t, and that’s okay,” John takes the torch from him, setting it on the counter so it shines up at the ceiling. “We can eat something else. We’ve got bread, and cheese. Crisps. You know I love crisps.”

“Too much,” he mutters.

“Yeah, well. Happy Valentine’s to me,” John tries a grin. “We’ll have a candlelit crisp tasting, it’s very classy, it’s what the kids are doing don’t you know?”

It’s stupid how easily John can pull him out of his own spiral. Maybe not all the way, but enough that he can breathe again. 

“Fine,” he says, rubbing his forehead. “We will get crisps.”

“And what’s next on the famous Dias schedule?”

“The museum,” he says with a little sigh. “I booked us a private tour. But they close at six, so if the power doesn’t come back…”

“We can still go, can’t we? Maybe they’ve got power.”

“Maybe,” he’s not hopeful, but nods anyway. “Finish your shower, I’ll call them.”

 

The museum, as it turns out, does not have power. It is closed, and the woman on the phone was very apologetic as she explains that their generators have failed too, Valentine’s special or no Valentine’s special. She offers to reschedule them, and he wants to bang his head against the wall. 

“That’s alright,” he says instead. “Thank you.”

He hangs up. The timer in his head, the whole invisible schedule he’s mapped out shudders and collapses.

John comes back into the kitchen, all dressed now and smelling like their shower gel – their, they smell the same now and Rúben doesn’t think he can get over that. He takes one look at Rúben’s face and opens his arms.

“C’mere, baby.”

He goes quickly, which he isn’t proud of. He folds himself into John’s chest. 

“What happened?” John murmurs into his hair.

“They’re closed. Power problems there too.”

“Shit,” John says. He pulls back a little. “So, museum’s dead, oven’s dead, lights dead. I reckon there’s a shop down the road that still has power, yeah? Let’s go get some snacks. We can have a picnic on the living room floor.”

They make their way down the stairs, because the lift’s out too. The street outside’s dark, but some places are bright while others are completely gone. The corner shop, luckily, is in the bright category.

“See?” John says. “Signs of civilisation.”

 

Inside, John takes the mission very seriously. He walks up and down the aisles, basket in one hand, choosing crisps and sandwiches like he’s picking a wine pairing. And by the time they step out of the shop, John has the bottle of prosecco tucked under one arm and two overstuffed bags swinging from his hand.

The sky’s still grey. Some windows in their block are still dark, but others are already growing yellow. Rúben’s eyes go straight to their own floor. 

“Hold on,” John says, squinting up. “Is that our bedroom light?”

It is. A soft square of warm light where, an hour ago, there’d been nothing. Rúben feels his shoulders sag with a mixture of relief and a bit of annoyance. Like, why’d they have to wait now?

“Looks like we’re back in the 21st century,” John says, nudging his elbow. 

Inside their flat, the hallway light finally clicks on when he hits the switch. The living room’s bright when he flicks that too, overhead lamp throwing everything into clear focus. 

If the power had come back half an hour earlier, the lasagna might’ve survived. The museum might’ve stayed open. Rúben’s Valentine’s day plans wouldn’t have gone into complete shit. 

John glances over, his smile dimming a little as he takes in Rúben’s face. “Hey. Don’t look like that.”

“I’m fine,” Rúben lies, setting the bags he has forced from John’s hands – because there was no way he’s letting his boy carry all that on his own – on the table. “We have power again. It’s good.”

John folds his arms. “You don’t look like a man having a good time right now.”

He exhales sharply, looking up at their ceiling. “It’s just stupid.”

“What’s stupid?”

“All of it,” he drags a hand through his hair, pacing a couple of steps and then back again. “I planned everything, I spent hours on the phone with that museum, I wanted it to be–”

He cuts himself off before he can say perfect. It feels childish in his mouth now.

“Special,” he finishes instead. “I wanted you to have something special, not just crappy crisps and wilted flowers and no electricity.”

John’s expression softens completely. He pushes off the counter and walks over, palms landing gentle and warm on Rúben’s chest. He tilts his head, then sighs, studying him for a second.

“Babe. You realise you’ve said that about a million times today, yeah? That you wanted it to be special, that you ruined it like it’s your fault the entire electrical system of Manchester decided to break down.”

“I didn’t say that,” Rúben grumbles.

“You implied it. With your very intense face.”

His thumbs are stroking back and forth now, unconsciously, over the fabric of Rúben’s shirt right where his heart is thumping. That alone calms him.

“I just wanted to show you I can do it,” he says, frustration slipping out. “It’s our first proper Valentine’s together, John, and I wanted to show you that I’m not just flowers and a card. And now the power comes back when it’s too late for everything and it feels like the day’s just wasted.”

He expects John to argue, tell him he’s a bit dramatic and laugh it off. Instead, though, John steps in closer right into his space, pressing their bodies together from chest to thigh.

“Can I tell you a secret?” he says, sliding one hand up. “I don’t really give a shit about Valentine’s Day.”

“That’s a lie.”

“No, I mean it,” John says. “I don’t care about the date. I don’t care about whether we’re sat in some fancy restaurant or on our floor with the box of chicken wings. Everyday with you already feels like Valentine’s.”

The words hit him straight in his chest. He swallows. “Everyday?” 

“Everyday,” John says simply. “You don’t have to prove anything to me, Rubes. I already know you love me. I feel it every bloody day.”

He’s quiet for a moment, breathing in John’s scent.

“You’re too good to me,” he says finally. 

“Yeah, well. Someone’s got to keep up with you,” John’s mouth curves. “You set the bar pretty high, Mister I Booked A Private Museum Tour.”

He huffs out a laugh, despite himself. “You would’ve liked it.”

“I’ll like it next week, when we go,” John says. “Sounds good?”

That pulls a softer smile out of him. “Sounds good.”

John watches it happen, something melting in his own face. “There he is,” he murmurs. “My dramatic little baby.”

“I’m not little.”

“Alright, my massive dramatic baby,” John corrects. “Better?”

They stand there, looking at each other. Then John’s gaze drops to his mouth. 

“D’you want to know another secret?” 

“Always.”

“I’ve been thinking about kissing you properly since we left training,” John says. “But you were so stressed about the Day of All Days I thought you’d combust if I tried anything.”

“You should’ve tried.”

“Oh, should I?” John’s mouth twitches. “You sure you can handle that now?”

John’s hands are already on his waist. Rúben leans in, mouth opening around John’s sigh like it’s something to drink from. Wet and slow, a little messy. John’s fingers tighten at his hips, gripping like he’d been waiting all day to do exactly this. Rúben pushes forward on instinct, angling John back until his spine bumps the edge of the kitchen counter. Their teeth clink once, and John grins against his mouth.

“Mm–eager now, aren’t we,” he whispers, a hand cupping Rúben’s jaw with tenderness. “Don’t think you’re getting away with how today went, though.”

“...getting away?”

“Oh yeah,” John says, tilting his head just enough to speak directly into his ear. “I think I’m going to punish you for the single worst Valentine’s Day this city’s ever seen.”

The change in his behaviour makes Rúben laugh a little bit, startled. “You’re punishing me?”

“Mhm,” John drawls, tongue flicking out to tease the shell of his ear, then his teeth scrapes it and makes Rúben twitch. “You. The man who promised homemade food and museum dates. You owe me, lover.”

Rúben really ought to protest. He’s not in the mood to hand over control, especially not after today went so spectacularly, comically wrong. But John knows how to peel away his resistance.

John doesn’t bother waiting for verbal agreement. He herds Rúben out of the kitchen with his body pressed close, one hand still splayed possessively over his hip. When they reach the bedroom, John pushes him down onto the bed with a palm on his chest. Rúben’s elbows catch his weight, but John’s already reaching for the bedside drawer.

“You know what’s coming,” John murmurs, digging for the silk ties and tossing them onto the mattress. “So, hands up, mister. And no whining.”

Rúben almost rolls his eyes, but instead he obeys, lying back. He lets John catch both wrists and tie them together. He tugs to test the knot, and John grins, climbing over him and straddling his hips.

“Always so in charge, aren’t you?” John says softly. “But not tonight. You can handle that, can you?”

Rúben opens his mouth, but the words get caught by John’s mouth on his, devouring the letters, tongue sliding in and demanding until Rúben is forced to chase his breath. John hooks his thumbs into the waistband of his boxers, yanking them down. 

He’s already hard, and John notices, one eyebrow cocked.

“You’re that easy,” he teases, then moves lower, mouth pressing hot, open kisses along Rúben’s stomach, tracing the V of his hips. He settles between his legs and lifts them with no ceremony, until Rúben’s ankles are braced on his shoulders.

There’s lube. It’s cold, making him gasp when John works two fingers in. Rúben bites the inside of his cheek, trying not to squirm, but John is relentless with his fingers, moving and stretching until the discomfort ebbs into heady fullness. 

Then John pulls back, and Rúben hears the unmistakable sound of the drawer opening again. Something smaller now – he doesn’t need to see it to know it. John presses the plug against him, pushes, and it slips inside with a pressure that has Rúben’s toes curling.

“There we go,” John whispers, palming his thigh. “Just relax for me, babe.”

And then John is moving, climbing up over him, his cock pressing insistently at his entrance. Rúben tenses on reflex, but John’s hands are so gentle. Then he pushes in, stretching him open in a way Rúben rarely allows. It burns at first, but John keeps moving, small thrusts.

“Fuck, you feel good,” he groans, rocking once, then twice, deep inside.

Rúben can’t hold in the sound that spills from his lips. John’s always more about making him feel every inch, every drag than chasing his own pleasure. He leans down, teeth scraping at Rúben’s jaw, kissing him hard then pulling back to watch every flicker of expression. 

John fucks him until he’s hard, cock pressed against his stomach, leaking and twitching with every thrust. Then, with a wicked gleam, he withdraws and replaces his own cock with another toy. It vibrates now, pressed just right inside of him. 

Rúben’s whole body jolts. “John––”

“Don’t you dare come yet,” John warns, but the words are all for show. 

He twists the toy, grinds it just so, and Rúben cries out, hips bucking. The pressure of his orgasm builds unbearably, and then before he knows it he’s coming untouched, legs shaking, wrists straining at the silk ties as he spills across his own stomach. 

“God––fuuuck––”

The orgasm hits him like a bolt. Rúben whimpers a half-broken sound again, hips jerking as his cock continues to pulse untouched, spent heat streaking his belly. He’s gasping for air, head tossed back against the pillow. Before he can even recover though, John’s hands are already working at the silk, his wrists expertly re-tied to the headboard. 

“Hey–” he starts, still catching his breath. “John, no–”

But John just grins, and this time he slips a blindfold over his eyes, Rúben’s world vanishing into hot velvet darkness.

He tenses, his senses sharpende by the loss of sight, every brush of John’s fingers magnified. The mattress dips as he feels John kneel between his legs. Rúben’s spent cock lies slick and soft against his thigh, and he flinches when John leans in, flicking a tongue over the head. A shudder bolts through him. He can’t see, but he feels it, the wet heat of John’s mouth, the teasing drag of his tongue circling the head and his nails buried deep into Rúben’s thighs, refusing to let him pull away. 

“John, it’s too much,” he groans, but John just hums as a response, wrapping his lips gently around the tip, sucking soft and slow. Rúben squirms again, wrists straining against the silk but John’s hands pin him down further, holding him still. 

“Stop moving.”

He mouths lower then, sucking Rúben’s balls into his mouth this time. It sends shocks through Rúben’s oversensitised nerves, his breath stuttering as John licks a broad stripe up his length, nuzzling, teasing, letting Rúben feel promise in his mouth. 

He shouldn’t be getting hard – he’s still shivering from the last orgasm – but John keeps working him, licking and sucking relentlessly until Rúben’s cock starts twitching again, the ache blooming into another desperate hardness.

Blind and bound, he can only pant, helpless to the slow torture as John drags his tongue around the crown this time, then takes him deep for a single, dizzying moment. 

“Fuck, John please if you want to suck me off just–”

He can’t finish. John laughs against him, still not saying a word. The bed then shifts – John moving, a pause, the scrape of the lube bottle, and then then suddenly there’s the blunt heat of him sinking down. The pressure makes Rúben groan, tensing his thigh.

John rides him, strong thighs bracketing Rúben’s hips. He can feel John’s hands planted on his chest, for balance. He moves with torturous patience, rising up until only the head is inside, then sinking back, grinding down in a rhythm that has Rúben gasping, back arching.

It’s too much. John’s hole is tight and hot around him, and he can fee the tremor in John’s muscles, every flex of his hips, every soft moan and curse spilling from John’s mouth. And it’s almost too much to bear, and Rúben’s cock is throbbing again. 

Just when the pressure’s built unbearably, just when Rúben’s right on the precipice, John lifts off completely. He leaves him exposed, empty, cock flushed and leaking and twitching uselesssly. 

“John, please, please–” Rúben chokes, so hard it hurts, unable to see, unable to touch, straining against the bonds. 

John’s mouth is at his ear, his voice unusually thick. “Not yet. You’re gonna wait for me, yeah?”

A low whimper escapes Rúben. He’s never felt so desperate.

Finally, when Rúben’s breath is ragged, his whole body shaking with want, John sinks back down, taking him in one smooth motion. This time, John rides him hard and fast, and the slap of skin and skin, his hands bracing, and all Rúben do is thrust up, up, up, blind and wild. 

“Come on, baby, give it to me,” John pants, rocking faster now, his own breath stuttering. 

Rúben breaks, his second orgasm of the night tearing out of him. A cry rips from his throat as he spills inside John. His boy groans above him, chasing his own climax with his hands, milking every last aftershock. 

He feels a body collapse against him, sweaty and loose. Rúben’s wrists are raw in the silk, every inch of him feels used, ruined in all the best ways. He barely even notices John shift until slick fingers press against his lips.

“Open.”

Rúben, already half-gone, parts his lips without thinking. John’s fingers push inside, slick with cum that’s trickled from him, salty and unmistakably Rúben’s. John presses under his jaw, making sure he doesn’t shy away as the fingers go deep just shy of choking. Rúben gags, helpless to stop the raw sound that claws from his throat. 

John finally withdraws. He wipes the mess across Rúben’s tongue once more, watches his face twist, then melt. It’s filthy, and it leaves him shivering all over. 

When it’s done, John leans in, his voice soft again. “Good boy. That’s my good boy.” 

The blindfold slips away, cool air on his sweaty face. The silk at his wrists loosens, falls away, and Rúben blinks into the bright, ordinary light of their bedroom. He can see John now, flushed, blue eyes blown wild, his thick lips bitten raw from kissing and biting. 

Rúben just stares at him. He’s dazed and ruined, and everything, everything comes surging up. His yes sting. He laughs a little.

“Fuck,” he says, trembling on the edge of a sob and another laugh. He surges up, catching John in his arms, burying his face in John’s shoulder. “God, I love you,” he blurts. “I fucking love you, John, I–” He breaks off, kissing him hard, again and again, his mouth frantic and arms tight around John’s shoulders “You’re everything. You’re–fuck, you’re everything to me. Doesn’t matter what goes wrong, if the world burns down, I just–God, I love you.”

John huffs out a soft, startled laugh, arms coming up to hold him just as tight, squeezing until it almost hurts. “Yeah?” he says, grinning against his mouth. “Yeah, you fucking better.”

Rúben laughs, breathless, face wet where he didn’t even realise he was crying, kissing him again. Softer this time, nothing but heat and gratitude and a love so sharp it hurts. He keeps kissing him until the frantic edge drains out of it, until it turns into slow presses and little sighs against John’s mouth like he’s trying to memorise the shape of him. John lets him. Johnl ets him do anything right now, because he’s warm and loose and safe, and also because he likes it when Rúben’s hands go gentle after they’ve been shaking.

 

They end up tangled together under the duvet. Rúben’s palm cups John’s cheek, still half in disbelief at the way the night flipped on its head. One second John is all teeth and confidence, the next he’s melted into Rúben’s chest.

“You alright?” Rúben asks.

John hums, then nods, rubbing his nose into Rúben’s throat. “Yeah. More than.” He pauses, then adds, softer, “Your wrists though.”

“My wrists?”

John shifts up to take Rúben’s hands, turning them over in the dim light, inspecing the faint mark with a tiny frown. He presses a kiss to one, then the other, then looks up with those stupid blue eyes.

“So my sweet boy’s back?” Rúben jokes before he can stop himself.

John’s face changes immediately, all shy and obedient. “I’m still your sweet boy?”

“Always,” Rúben says, and it comes out rougher than he expects. He slides a hand to the back of John’s head and holds him there. “Always and forever.” Rúben kisses the tip of his nose. “I don’t get you, you now. The… how can you be–” He searches for the word, “how can you wreck me and then cuddle up like you’re made of… marshmallows?”

John grins, smug, then he nuzzles closer again. “I just like being with you. It makes my brain go quiet.”

Rúben drops a kiss on John’s hair, then his temple, then the side of his face. “Even though nothing went right today.”

John tips his chin up. “Wrong. This went right. You went right. I’m right here, I didn’t go anywhere. It was a great first Valentine’s, Ruby.”

Rúben swallows, because that’s the whole thing, isn’t it? He brushes John’s cheek with his knuckles and watches him soften even more, eyelids growing heavy. 

“Come closer,” Rúben whispers.

John does, immediately. He curls into him with a little sigh, and Rúben feels the punch to his heart, the sweetest kind. He presses his mouth to John’s ear and smiles into his skin.

“Happy Valentines, baby.”
